mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that lowers discount rates for equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL > 3) signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO > 100) and housing starts (HOUST > 1500) signals robust supply-side expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of INDPRO and HOUST on SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in any 3-year rolling window, or individual series show negative correlations.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightening (UNRATE < 3.5% and PAYEMS > 150k) signals wage inflation pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns during late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE < 3.5 and PAYEMS > 150k, or the interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS is not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) indicates resilient labor demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days regardless of macro conditions.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in consumer demand (RSAFS < 680k) signals weak private consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS falls below 680k, or the correlation between RSAFS and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1 > 2%) and housing starts (HOUST > 1500) indicates strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and HOUST on SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in any 3-year rolling window, or individual series show negative correlations.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS > 3.5%) signals higher discount rates for equities, leading to negative S&P 500 returns during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
